observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous years’ recommendations, and the Local Chief
Executive agreed to ensure that the: (a) Municipal Treasurer improve collection
strategies on RPT and initiate the available remedial measures in collecting the
overdue taxes from delinquent taxpayers; and (b) Municipal Accountant set up
the RPT/SET Receivables at the beginning of the year based on the Certified List
of Taxpayers and Tax Due prepared by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer.

We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the (a) GSO/Supply
Division to maintain Stock/Property Cards for each commodity/stock in
compliance with the required maintenance of complete and updated inventory
records; and (b) Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain Supplies Ledger
Cards (SLCs) for each commodity/stock to provide competent evidence to
document the accuracy of the cost of inventories.

In view of the above observations, we recommended and Management
agreed, that the (a) Inventory Committee and Property Officer (i) prioritize the
conduct of physical inventory of the Municipality’s PPE in accordance with the
guidelines provided under paragraphs 6.1 and 6.2 of COA Circular No. 2020-006;
(ii) render an accurate inventory report as basis of the Accounting Unit to correct
the reported PPE balances in the Financial Statements; and (iii) prepare an
Inventory and Inspection Report on Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) for PPE
items identified as unserviceable/beyond economic repair and due for disposal; (b)
Accounting Office and GSO to (i) undertake collaborative efforts to reconcile the
RPCPPE with the books of accounts following the guidelines provided under
paragraph 6.3 of the aforementioned COA Circular; (ii) proceed with the
disposition and/or derecognition of the non-existing/missing items of PPE guided
by paragraphs 7.0 and 8.0 of the same COA Circular; and (iii) subsequently
conduct reconciliation on a regular basis to maintain the accuracy of reported
items of PPE; and (c) Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments
on the overstated accumulated depreciation and PPE accounts and subsequently
compute the depreciation expense for each item of PPE pursuant to Section 4,
Chapter 2 of NGAS Manual for LGUs, Vol. I and Item 4.0 of COA Circular No.
2003-007.

We recommended and Management agreed to continue its compliance with
the guidelines outlined in Section 50 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I, to
ensure the fair presentation of the balances in the affected accounts within the
financial statements.

We recommended, and the Local Chief Executive agreed that the
Municipal Accountant (i) ensure strict adherence to the reporting and
liquidation timelines stipulated in COA Circular No. 94-013, the MOA and
Specific Implementation Agreements; and (ii) conduct regular and prompt
monitoring and reconciliation of complementary accounts to ensure accurate
financial reporting and compliance with existing rules and regulations.

We recommended, and Management agreed to: a) continue its adherence
to Section 287 of RA No. 7160, as well as DBM-DOF-DILG Joint Memorandum
Circular (JMC) No. 1 dated November 4, 2020 regarding the appropriation and
utilization of the 20% Development Fund; b) submit the approved Municipal
Appropriation Ordinance/Sangguniang Bayan Resolution to support the excess
obligation amounting to ₱681,666.00; and c) abide strictly with the prioritization
of projects embodied in the 5-year comprehensive development plan in compliance
with item 3.2.1 of the same JMC, or require revising the CDP.

We recommended, and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the
Management to continue its efforts to maintain substantial compliance with the
guidelines governing the appropriation, utilization, and accounting of the
LDRRMF, pursuant with the rules and regulations stipulated in RA No. 10121,
DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 dated March 25, 2013, and
COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended, and the Local Chief Executive agreed to ensure that the
(a) Municipal Treasurer and BPLO : (i) maintain strict enforcement on the
submission of Audited Financial Statements, or, in their absence, Sworn
Statements of Gross Receipts/Sales or copies of Income Tax Returns from
taxpayers, for the accurate computation of tax due; (ii) ensure the sustained
implementation and enforcement of the Municipal Revenue Code; and (iii)
consistently apply the prescribed tax rates according to the taxpayer's line of
business.

In view of the above audit observations, we recommended and the Local
Chief Executive and the GFPS agreed to continue implementing the proven and
effective methods to sustain their pursuit of gender equality and increased
awareness of gender issues and women's empowerment.

We recommended, and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the: (i)
Head of the BAC Secretariat to furnish the Audit Team with certified copies of
Contracts together with its supporting documents within the prescribed period;
(ii) Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to strategize effective
measures on how to minimize, if not eliminate the incurrence of delays in the
submission of accounts; (iii) Municipal Accountant to (a) require other officials
concerned in the processing and arrangement of DVs with deficiencies to effect
prompt submission thereof to her office; and (b) ensure the submission to the
Audit Team of the paid DVs and supporting documents, Trial Balances, and Bank
Reconciliation Statements, within the prescribed period (c) ensure that certified
copies of Contracts were duly received by COA before certifying the completeness
of supporting documents and signing in box A of the Disbursement Vouchers; and
(iv) Municipal Budget Officer promptly submit the monthly SAOBs to the Audit
Team.

We recommended, and the Local Chief Executive agreed to ensure that the
Municipal Human Resources Management Officer (MHRMO) (i) require all JO
personnel to prepare and submit quantifiable and measurable individual
accomplishment reports, duly reviewed by their supervisors, and ensure that these
reports are attached to the payroll documents to comply with the documentary
requirements outlined in COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012; (ii) to the
extent practicable, require the preparation of a duly approved and notarized
contract for each JO employee upon hiring, clearly stipulating their specific duties
and responsibilities, which can then be cross-referenced with their
accomplishment reports; and, (iii) facilitate coordination with Department Heads
to ascertain the necessity of hiring JO personnel in each department, thereby
maximizing the services of permanent employees.

We commended the Municipality for its compliance with the revenue
regulations, particularly on the proper withholding and prompt remittance of taxes
to the BIR. We also recommended the Municipality’s continued adherence to these
practices.

We recommended and Management agreed to continue complying with the
provisions of RA No. 8291 and its IRR, particularly on the prompt remittance to
the GSIS of the premium contributions and loan amortizations withheld from the
employees’ compensation.

We commended the Management for its religious compliance with the Home
Development Mutual Fund Law of 2009 on the proper withholding of premiums and
loan amortizations of its employees and the prompt remittance of the same,
together with the Municipality’s share to the HDMF. We also recommended and
Management agreed, that said compliance would be continuously practiced.

We recommended, and Management agreed to continue its adherence with
the National Health Insurance Act of 2013 RA 10606) and its Revised Implementing
Rules and Regulations to ensure the ongoing health insurance coverage of its
employees.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the (a) BPLO to: (i) strictly enforce the submission of Audited FS or in its absence, the Sworn Statement of Gross Receipts/ Sales or a copy of ITR from taxpayers for the proper computation of deficiency tax, if any; (ii) be stricter in the implementation and enforcement of the Municipal Revenue Code; (iii) ensure that the prescribed rates are correctly applied based on the line of business of the taxpayer; (b) Municipal Treasurer to examine and verify the books of accounts and other pertinent records of any business establishments; and (c) BPLO and Municipal Treasurer to verify, assess, and validate other businesses aside from the two noted businesses and demand the payment of computed under collection of business taxes.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation and Management
agreed that the: (a) Municipal Accountant to set-up the RPT/SET Receivables at
the beginning of the year based on the Certified List of Taxpayers and Tax Due
prepared by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer; and (b) Municipal Treasurer
to improve collection strategies on RPT and initiate the available remedial
measures in collecting the overdue taxes from delinquent taxpayers.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the: (a) Municipal
Accountant and the GSO to complete the necessary procedures enumerated in
COA Circular No. 2020-006 on the missing/unlocated PPE items and
unidentifiable balances; (b) Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary
journal entry to record the donated machineries; and (c) Disposal Committee to
facilitate the disposal of the remaining unserviceable PPEs in accordance with the
prescribed guidelines and procedures.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the (a) BPLO to: (i)
strictly enforce the submission of Audited FS or in its absence, the Sworn
Statement of Gross Receipts/ Sales or a copy of ITR from taxpayers for the proper
computation of deficiency tax, if any; (ii) be more strict in the implementation and
enforcement of the Municipal Revenue Code; (iii) ensure that the prescribed rates
are correctly applied based on the line of business of the taxpayer; (b) Municipal
Treasurer to examine and verify the books of accounts and other pertinent records
of any business establishments; and (c) BPLO and Municipal Treasurer to verify,
assess, and validate other businesses aside from the two noted businesses and
demand the payment of computed under collection of business taxes.

We commended the Management on its compliance with DBM LBC No.
144 dated February 28, 2022 on the implementation and utilization of the NTF-
ELCAC Fund and encouraged the LGU officials to continue its good performance
on the timely implementation of projects and prompt liquidation of fund transfers
received from National Government Agencies.

We recommended and Management agreed to: (a) continue its substantial
compliance with Section 287 of R.A. No. 7160 or the Local Government Code of
1991 and DBM, DOF and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 1, dated
November 4, 2020 on the appropriation and utilization of 20% Development
Fund; and (b) update the Comprehensive Development Plan consistent with item
3.2.1 of the same Circular.

We recommended and Management agreed to continue its efforts to
substantially comply with the guidelines on the appropriation, utilization, and
accounting of LDRRMF following the rules and regulations under RA No. 10121
or the Philippine Disaster Risk Reduction Management Act of 2010 and COA
Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

In view of the above audit observations, we recommended and
Management agreed to: (a) continue to adhere with the provisions of RA No. 9003
or the SWM Act of 2000 on the appropriation and implementation of its SWM
Program; (b) rigorously implement the Municipal Ordinances in relation to SWM
and impose sanctions to violators, if warranted; and (c) take appropriate action to
expedite the request for assistance from the donor agency on the use of donated
machineries to make them operational.

For the aforesaid deficiencies, we recommended that Management: (a)
strictly comply with the allocation of the prescribed budget for GAD pursuant to
Republic Act No. 9710 or the Magna Carta of Women; (b) instruct the GFPS to
monitor the implementation of GAD PPAs in accordance with GAD Plan and
Budget as reviewed by the DILG; (c) issue Executive Order to comply with the
required structure and composition of GFPS; and (d) instruct the GFPS to create
a centralized GAD database that would consolidate the information gathered and
compiled by all offices to include gender statistics and other relevant sex-
disaggregated data, as basis in GAD planning, programming and policy making.

We recommended and Management agreed to continue the practice of
complying with the proper withholding and full remittance of taxes due the BIR
in accordance with Section 114 (c) of the National Revenue Code as amended by
RA No. 9337 and Revenue Regulation No. 2-98.

We recommended and Management agreed to continue to perform the
agency’s mandate as withholding agent of the government with emphasis on strict
observance of regulation on timely and intact remittances of employees’ and
employers’ contributions in accordance with Section 6 (b) of Republic Act No.
8291 dated May 30, 1997.

intact and timely remittances of employees’ and employers’ contributions due to

practice of remitting Pag-IBIG premium contributions on a timely basis so that
the employees can enjoy all the privileges of a Pag-IBIG member.

Executive direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile
their records of Real Property Tax (RPT) and SEF receivables in order to report
reliably the said account in the financial statements. Moreover, we recommended
that the LCE instruct the Municipal Treasurer to improve collection strategies on

the audit recommendations.

2.26 For the deficiencies discussed above, we reiterated and Management agreed
to: (a) instruct the Municipal Accountant and GSO, to (i) exert more efforts in
locating documents pertinent to the acquisition of its PPEs, maintain their respective
PPE records, and thereafter, conduct at least on a quarterly basis, the reconciliation
of their PPE information; (b) instruct the Municipal Assessor to facilitate the
registration and obtain titles for all real properties over which the Municipality has
interest and ownership; and (c) consider the one-time cleaning of PPE account
balances to improve the reliability of the PPE accounts.

3.11 In view of the foregoing, we recommended and Management agreed to: (a)
ensure the strict compliance with the guidelines on the receipt and utilization of trust
funds; (b) revert/return the trust fund for which the intended purpose could no longer
be pursued or no longer needed since unwarranted retention of these funds deprives
the donor agencies to remobilize the fund to other activities or in other agencies
needing assistance; and (c) concerned officials to be more prudent in the
implementation of the programs, projects and activities so that constituents can enjoy
the benefits derivable therefrom.

4.9 We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal
Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to resolve the issues on the delays in
releasing the Barangays shares on RPT collections and henceforth, observe adherence
on the quarterly releases of the same.

5.10 We recommended and Management agreed to: (a) continue to comply with
Section 287 of the RA 7160 and DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No.1 dated November 4, 2020
on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% DF; and (b) ensure that all
procurements are in compliance with the approved APP as required under RA No.
9184.

6.8 We recommended and Management agreed to: (a) appoint a Project
Facilitator who shall assist in the Pakyaw Groups and preparation of pakyaw
contracts, convene community meetings relative to the project and organization of
pakyaw groups through registration of unemployed and underemployed members of
the community; (b) instruct the management to draw Pakyaw Labor from the vicinity
of the project, or pakyaw labor groups that belong to various Barangay Association
contiguous to the project site; (c) specify in the pakyaw contracts the skill category of
each pakyaw group whether skilled, semi-skilled, or unskilled and indicate their exact
residence for easy and future reference; and (d) strictly comply with the provision of
Section 4.8 of Appendix 1 of RIRR of RA No. 9184 in the succeeding implementation
of the projects through Pakyaw Contracting System.

7.15 We recommended and Management agreed to: (a) comply with the guidelines
set forth by R.A. No. 10121 and NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 2013-
1; (b) consider the execution during the budget year of the prioritized disaster
mitigation projects; and (c) require the MDRRM Officer and the Municipal
Accountant to observe the reporting for LDRRM activities as provided for under
NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 and COA Circular
No. 2012-002.

8.21 For the aforesaid deficiencies, we recommended and Management agreed to:
(i) continue to comply with the allocation of the prescribed budget for GAD and the
preparation of a relevant GAD Budget and Plan pursuant to the pertinent laws and
regulations on GAD; (b) instruct the GFPS to monitor the implementation of GAD
PPAs in accordance with GAD Plans and Budget as reviewed by the DILG; (c) issue
Executive Order to comply with the required structures and composition of GFPS;
(d) instruct the GFPS to create a centralized GAD database that would consolidate
the information gathered and compiled by all offices to include gender statistics and
other relevant sex-disaggregated data, as basis in GAD planning, programming and
policy making; and (d) timely submit to the Audit Team the GAD Accomplishment
Report in compliance with COA Circular No. 2014-001.

10.3 We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer and the Accountant agreed to
continue the practice of complying with the withholding and remittance of taxes due
the BIR in accordance with Section 114 (c) of the National Revenue Code as amended
by RA No. 9337 and Revenue Regulation No. 2-98.

11.3 We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer and the OIC-Municipal
Accountant agreed to continue the practice of the strict observance of GSIS
regulation on withholding of employee’s contributions and timely remittances of
employees’ and employers’ contributions in accordance with Section 6 (b) of Republic
Act No. 8291 dated May 30, 1997.

Accountant agreed to continue the practice of timely remittances of employees’ and
employers’ contributions due the PhilHealth in accordance with Section 18 of the IRR
of the National Health Insurance Act of 2013 and PhilHealth Circular No. 1, s. 2014.

13.2 We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer and the OIC-Municipal
Accountant agreed to continuously observe the practice of remitting Pag-IBIG
premium contributions on a timely basis so that the employees can enjoy all the
privileges of a Pag-IBIG member.

14.5 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to consistently
maintain the observance of the accounting and auditing rules and regulations,
particularly in ensuring the regularity, validity, completeness of documentation of the
transactions, among others, to prevent the incurrence of similar deficiencies that
resulted in the issuance of audit suspension.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct –

a.	 the Municipal Social Welfare Officer (MSWO) to be more diligent in the preparation, review of payrolls based on the approved list of qualified beneficiaries and implementation of financial assistance of similar nature in the future to avoid incurrence of similar lapses;

b.	the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant, in the implementation of similar projects in the future, to 

         (a) closely coordinate with the MSWO in monitoring and facilitating the timely preparation of necessary documents, such as checks, encashment, other related action which are necessary in expediting the delivery of financial assistance to beneficiaries; and 
           (b) consistently observe, monitor compliance on timely  submission of liquidation reports and refund, if any, of unutilized fund to the source agency to help maximize the timely and efficient utilization of limited government fund.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the designated officer responsible for the receipt, utilization, distribution and inventory of cash and in-kind donations to: 

(a) comply with Section II.1 (a) of the COA Circular No. 2020-09 by accounting all donations received during quarantine period; and 

(b) to submit all the required supporting documents of the donations received from all the donors.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to instruct:

A.	The Municipal Treasurer to:

(a)	introduce collection strategy that would significantly help realize the collection of prior years’ RPT/SET receivables of the Municipality; 



(b)	coordinate with the Accounting Office for the reconciliation of their respective records and to effect the necessary adjustments/corrections; 



(c)	ensure that the list of taxpayers bear vital information, such as the exact addresses, to facilitate, if necessary, the sending of notices and other follow up communications relative to tax campaign activities and other collection strategy that may be implemented by Management; and

(d)	use available remedies, administrative and/ or judicial action, for the collection of taxes as provided in Section 256 of the Local Government Code of 1991.

B.	The Municipal Accountant to: 

(a)	observe the provision under Section 20 of the New Government Accounting (NGAS) Manual, as amended by Section 42 of Government Accounting Manual (GAM), Volume I, by using the Certified List of Taxpayers and Tax Due prepared by the Office of the Treasurer as the basis in recording the RPT/SEF tax receivables at the beginning of the year; and

(b)	coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer’s Office for the reconciliation of their respective records and to effect the necessary adjustments/ corrections in the reported balances of the affected accounts, if any. 

C.	The Municipal Assessor to coordinate and timely provide the MTO with the needed assessment reports to facilitate the preparation of Certified List of Taxpayers by the latter within the prescribed period. 

Further, include in the programs/ projects/ activities (PPAs) the conduct of tax/land mapping to update the assessor’s records on real properties in order to come up with a more reliable basis in setting up of the subject receivable account.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) designate permanent personnel who will be responsible in obtaining water permit and water test analysis from the National Water Resources Board and authorized Water District Laboratory, respectively, and (b) submit copies of the said permit and test analysis to COA office, to attain the objectives of the SALINTUBIG program and prevent the deterioration and wastage of government funds and properties pursuant to Section 2 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The concerned Municipal Officials agreed to formulate strategies and policies for the operation of SALIN-TUBIG projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive, the Vice Mayor and the Sanggunian concerned agreed to (a) provide appropriation/funds for the titling of the four (4) lots in the name of the Municipal Government of Licab; and (b) designate personnel/s who will undertake the titling thereof to ensure the right of ownership over the subject real properties.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to ensure that sound internal control system in the recording and accounting of revenues is strictly adhered by directing the Acting Municipal Accountant to (a) reconcile the RPT/SET receivables and revenues/collection records with that of the Provincial Accountant by obtaining the complete descriptions of collections corresponding to the remittances made therefrom for the period CY 2016 to CY 2018; and (b) effect the necessary adjusting entries to recognize the ₱44,772.20 and other understatements caused by lack of supporting statements, to reflect the correct amount of RPT/SET Receivable and Deferred RPT/SET at year end. Relatively, we also enjoined the Municipal Treasurer to cause the postings of individual payment of the taxpayers to their respective RPTARs based on statements covered by the remittances of the Province on RPT/SET.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to cause the allotment of funds for the provision of municipal facilities and assignment of permanent personnel to secure and monitor the whereabouts and utilization of municipal vehicles and equipment pursuant to Section 2 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer to insure all insurable assets of the Municipality of Licab to the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS to comply with Republic Act No. 656 as amended by P.D. No. 245 dated July 13, 1973.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to (a) ensure the strict adherence to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445 regarding complete documentation of claims against government funds; (b) direct the Municipal accountant to review the completeness and validity of the supporting documents of the claim before certifying the Disbursement Vouchers and allowing its payment; and (c) require the concerned Municipal Officials to submit the necessary supporting documents to prove the validity of the claim.

We recommended and the OIC Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to continue the practice of timely and full remittance of the total taxes withheld every month in compliance with the BIR Regulations.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer and the OIC Municipal Accountant agreed to continue the practice of remitting amounts due the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS) within the prescribed period in adherence to Section 6 (b) of Republic Act No.  8291.

We recommended and the Acting Municipal Accountant agreed to notify the personnel concerned to settle the suspensions thru submission of the required supporting documents. The required supporting documents for the suspended transactions be likewise submitted to the Acting Municipal Accountant for proper recording, and subsequently to the Auditor for adjustment of records.

We recommended and the incumbent Municipal Mayor agreed to ensure the strict adherence to MNGAS pursuant to Chapter 2, Section 2.1 of PPSAS by          (a) designating a capable permanent official/employee as Property Officer who shall be responsible in locating the missing properties and submission of report thereon; (b)  determining the accountable officials therefor and cause the submission of request for relief from accountability, if warranted, otherwise cause the refund equivalent to the replacement cost of the missing PPEs; and (c) instructing the OIC-Municipal Accountant and the OIC-Property Officer to observe the procedures relative to the proper receipt, recording and reporting of the PPE accounts. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Committee who will assist the property officer in completing the physical count of PPE and in the preparation of Inventory Report thereon, to establish the correctness of the recorded Property, Plant and Equipment accounts.
observations.managementAction: Management adhered to the provisions of MNGAS.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer to exert more effort in conducting massive tax campaigns and information drives in the different barangays and effect the postings of Notice of Delinquency to each barangay of the Municipality to inform and motivate the people in payment of their tax obligations, in compliance with Section 254 (a) of the Local Government Code. We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to: (a) coordinate efforts with the concerned Sangguniang Bayan to pass municipal ordinances that would enforce the implementation of the five basic environmental laws on the preservation and protection of the environment required under R.A. No. 9003; (b) request the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to prioritize the finalization of the ten-year SWMP for the early identification of issues/gaps in complying with RA No. 9003 and the prompt resolution thereof; and (c) to prioritize in CY 2016 the unimplemented solid waste management.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) cause the allocation of at least five percent (5%) of the total appropriations for GAD related issues and concerns to be integrated in regular programs and activities of the Municipality, and (b) direct the Municipal Budget Officer to formulate GAD Plan in conformity with DILG, DBM and NCRFW Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2001-01 and COA Circular No.2014-001 and submit the same for review and approval of PCW and DBM.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to (a) strictly comply with the regulation on the rendition of accounts and financial reports in order not to incur a delay in the verification of accounts and in conducting post-audit activities; and (b) observe the due dates of the required financial reports and documents to the Audit Team.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to ensure the strict compliance to COA Circular Nos. 97-002 and 2009-002 relative to liquidation of cash advance within the period prescribed in the said Circular.
